# CPython 3.14t's biased-reference-counting object stack is part of the
# uninstrumented setup-python runtime. An externally preloaded TSan runtime
# cannot observe its internal atomic synchronization and reports allocator
# reuse between _PyObjectStackChunk_New and _PyObjectStack_Pop as a race.
# Keep these scoped to exact CPython frames; reports in sklearnex/daal4py/
# onedal frames remain fatal.
race:_PyObjectStack_Pop
race:ucs1lib_rfind_char

# oneDAL itself is supplied as an uninstrumented binary. Argument::set performs
# one side of a correct atomic SharedPtr decrement inside libonedal_core, which
# TSan cannot observe, then dispatches its inline deleter in the instrumented
# extension. Suppress only that exact HomogenNumericTable<double> control-block
# destructor; other oneDAL/daal4py frames remain fatal.
race:RefCounterImp<daal::services::interface1::ObjectDeleter<daal::data_management::interface1::HomogenNumericTable<double>>>::~RefCounterImp
